title: 10 best herbal decoctions for itchy eyes
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for itchy eyes are a natural and effective remedy to soothe and relieve irritation caused by dryness, allergies, or infections. By infusing herbs in hot water, these decoctions create a gentle and soothing liquid that can be applied directly to the affected area, providing instant relief from itching, burning, and redness. Examples of herbal decoctions that help with itchy eyes include chamomile, calendula, and peppermint, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that calm the eye surface and reduce swelling. For those who suffer from chronic dry eyes or seasonal allergies, these decoctions can greatly improve their quality of life by providing long-lasting relief and allowing them to enjoy everyday activities without discomfort or distraction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower decoctions helps with itchy eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The decoction's active compounds, such as ursolic acid and rosmarinic acid, work to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, while also suppressing the release of histamine, a chemical that can exacerbate itchiness. Additionally, the decoction's soothing properties help to calm irritated conjunctiva and provide fast relief from itchy, burning sensations. As a result, purple coneflower decoctions offer a natural and effective solution for alleviating itchy eyes.

Antihistamine: The flavonoids and polyphenols present in purple coneflower decoctions can inhibit the release of histamines, a chemical mediator responsible for allergic reactions, including itchy eyes.

Antioxidant: Purple coneflower decoctions are rich in antioxidants like quercetin, which can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, contributing to dry, itchy eyes by protecting the eyes' delicate tissues from damage.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea purpurea are commonly used to make decoctions due to their high concentration of alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that help to soothe itchy eyes.
Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea purpurea are often used in decoctions for their ability to reduce inflammation and relieve itching in the eyes.
Flowers: The flowers of Echinacea purpurea are used in decoctions to take advantage of their antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea roots or flowers according to your preference.
Chop 20-30 grams of dried roots or 30-40 grams of fresh roots or flowers into small pieces.
Combine the chopped plant material with 1 liter of boiling water in a large pot.
Simmer the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction and discard the solids before serving the herbal tea warm.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy eyes that are treated with herbal decoctions are conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and dry eye syndrome. Decoctions made from herbs such as licorice root, chamomile, and calendula help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing inflammation and itching. supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal decoctions that help with itchy eyes is a blend of calendula, eyebright, and licorice root. Calendula soothes and calms the skin around the eyes, while eyebright reduces inflammation and eases itching. Licorice root provides anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate redness and discomfort. Steep in hot water, then strain and let cool before applying with a cotton pad to the affected area.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Medicina (Kaunas, Lithuania), herbal decoctions may be beneficial for itchy eyes due to their anti-inflammatory properties. The study used a dry eye disease (DED) mouse model and found that after 3 months of treatment with a decoction of five traditional medicines, including Lycium chinense, Cuscuta chinensis, Senna tora, Ophiopogon japonicus, and Dendrobium nobile, epithelial erosions and desquamation were reduced, the intact corneal endothelium was maintained, and tear osmolarity was restored in the eyes. The IL-1β-associated inflammatory response was also reduced in the cornea, suggesting that herbal decoctions can help alleviate ocular irritation and discomfort associated with itchy eyes.

The mucilage in particular forms a protective barrier on the surface of the eyes, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort. Additionally, Symphytum officinale has been traditionally used to treat eye conditions, making it a popular natural remedy for soothing itchy eyes and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_constituents: Allantoin: It helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the eyes, alleviating itchiness associated with irritation.
Flavonoids: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to calm and protect the eyes from further irritation, reducing itchiness.
Saponins: They possess anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help to reduce swelling and itchiness in the eyes, promoting overall comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make decoctions for itchy eyes because they contain compounds like allantoin and mucilage, which help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Leaves: They are used to make decoctions for itchy eyes because they contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Flowers: They are used to make decoctions for itchy eyes because they contain mucilage, which helps to protect and lubricate the eyes, reducing irritation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh symphytum officinale leaves and roots in late spring or early summer for optimal potency.
Clean and chop 2-3 grams of the harvested symphytum officinale roots and 5-6 grams of leaves.
Combine the chopped roots and leaves in 200 milliliters of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Reduce heat to a simmer and continue steeping the mixture for an additional 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction and discard the solids after the total preparation time of 20-25 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea decoctions helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The herbal extract has been traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated eyes. The presence of compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid in Echinacea purpurea may help reduce inflammation and alleviate itchy sensations. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties may help combat underlying infections that could be causing eye irritation. This natural remedy may provide relief for people suffering from itchy eyes due to allergies, dryness, or other irritants.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from itchy eyes.
Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe and protect the eyes from irritation, reducing itchiness.
Cichoric acid: A phenolic compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, it can help reduce inflammation and alleviate itchiness in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Echinacea purpurea are often used to make decoctions for itchy eyes due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea purpurea are used to make decoctions for itchy eyes due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots of Echinacea purpurea are used to make decoctions for itchy eyes due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help reduce swelling and ease discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20-30 grams of dried echinacea purpurea roots and flowers or 40-60 grams of fresh plant material.
Crush the plant material into small pieces using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder.
Combine the crushed plant material with 500-750 milliliters of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 10-30 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup or container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis decoctions helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel extracted from the plant's leaves contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which reduce swelling and calm the irritated tissues. The decoction, when applied to the eyes, provides relief from itching and redness by reducing the production of histamine, a chemical responsible for allergic reactions. As a result, itchy eyes can experience significant relief and comfort. This natural remedy can be an effective solution for itchy eyes caused by allergies or irritants.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: Help reduce inflammation and soothe itchy eyes by neutralizing free radicals and inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Triterpenoids (specifically Aloe-emodin): Have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, reducing eye irritation and infection that can cause itchiness.
Glucomannans (specifically Acemannan): Have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, helping to calm and protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from irritation and itchiness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Aloe barbadensis due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloe-emodin and aloin, which help soothe itchy eyes.
Buds: The buds are used to make decoctions for itchy eyes because they contain a high amount of bioactive compounds, including saponins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Gel from the Leaves: Note: Since the question asked for a part, and the gel is a part of the leaves, I included this part in the list.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a minimum of 10 large aloe barbadensis leaves with thick green rinds for a 1-liter decoction.
Cut the leaves into 2-3 inch pieces and soak them in cold water for 2 hours to rehydrate.
Drain and puree the soaked aloe pieces in a blender or food processor for 30 seconds to 1 minute.
Combine the pureed aloe with 1 liter of boiling water and simmer for 30-40 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh sieve into a clean container to remove solids.

Flowers: The flowers contain hyperforin and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making them effective in treating itchy eyes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-30 grams of fresh or dried flowers and leaves of hypericum perforatum in late summer.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place with good air circulation for 1-2 weeks.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces and store them in airtight containers to preserve freshness.
Combine 1 teaspoon of chopped hypericum perforatum with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes and then strain the decoction before consuming it as a remedy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomilla recutita decoctions helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Luteolin: A flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antihistaminic properties, which help to soothe and calm itchy, irritated eyes.

remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used to make decoctions for itchy eyes because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make decoctions for itchy eyes because they contain apigenin and other flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh or 40-60 grams of dried flowers at dawn when the dew is still present.
Rinse the flowers with cold water to remove any debris or impurities from the environment.
Combine the flowers with 200 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant container or teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes to allow the bioactive compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container for consumption.

Leaves: The leaves are used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce itching and inflammation in the eyes.
Barks: The barks are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and itching, making them a common ingredient in traditional remedies for itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried root of glycyrrhiza glabra from a trusted source.
Rinse the root in cold water to remove any impurities or debris thoroughly.
Combine the root with 1 liter of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil slowly.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and let it steep for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
